title: Sampling and Extraction of Lyophilized Plant Tissue for LC-MS Analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7s10s","text":"This protocol outlines a standardized procedure for the collection, processing, and extraction of plant tissues for downstream LC-MS metabolite analysis. The method includes sterile sampling of fresh plant material in the field, immediate cryopreservation on dry ice, lyophilization, grinding to a fine powder using an IKA mill, and a two-step solvent extraction optimized for polar metabolites. The extracts are then clarified and transferred into LC-MS vials for analysis. This protocol ensures sample integrity and reproducibility across multiple plant tissue types, including leaf, stem, root, flower, and fruit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ovnd","text":"Ensure that all equipment (e.g., TissueLyser, SpeedVac, centrifuge, ultrasonic bath) is clean, calibrated, and functioning properly before starting the protocol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1khe","text":"Use pre-labeled tubes and vials throughout the procedure to maintain sample traceability.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1etj","text":"Handle all samples consistently to minimize biological and technical variability, especially during sampling and extraction steps.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9kaf1","text":"Sampling kit assembled (sterile instruments, gloves, ethanol, dry ice, labeled tubes).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4u0cl","text":"All solvents freshly prepared or verified.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9v5kj","text":"Instruments powered, clean, and pre-calibrated (balance, vortex, centrifuge, SpeedVac, etc.)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a60bs","text":"Extraction workspace is clear, ventilated, and equipped with proper waste containers.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4iejf","text":"Ethanol (EtOH) and acetonitrile (CH₃CN) are flammable solvents—keep away from open flames or heat sources. Always wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE), including lab coat, safety goggles, and nitrile gloves.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2skr1","text":"During TissueLyser and IKA mill operation, ensure the device is secured and the samples are balanced to prevent mechanical accidents.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"btfgt","text":"Handle dry ice with insulated gloves and avoid direct skin contact to prevent frostbite.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vqpp","text":"Dispose of solvent waste according to your institution’s chemical safety regulations.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Cut a defined portion of fresh plant tissue (e.g., leaf, root, stem, flower, or fruit) and immediately transfer it into a sterile 50 mL conical centrifuge tube.
Immediately place the tubes on dry ice to preserve sample integrity during transport to the laboratory.
Cover the opening of the tube with a single piece of fine mesh fabric to allow airflow while preventing contamination.
Secure the mesh fabric over the tube opening using a rubber band to ensure it remains firmly in place.
Place the prepared tubes into the lyophilizer flasks, ensuring a stable and upright position to facilitate efficient drying.
Perform lyophilization for a duration of 3 days, following the manufacturer’s operating guidelines for optimal drying efficiency.
Transfer the lyophilized plant material into an analytical mill and grind to a fine, homogeneous powder.
Store the ground plant material in a tightly sealed Falcon tube under dry room conditions to prevent moisture absorption.
Weigh 25 ± 2.5 mg of lyophilized plant material into a 2.0 mL round-bottom Eppendorf tube.
Add one stainless steel grinding bead to each tube to facilitate homogenization.
Homogenize the samples using a TissueLyser at 25 rpm for 30 seconds .
Add 2 000 μL of EtOH-H 2 O solution (75:25, v/v) to each tube and vortex briefly to ensure thorough mixing.
Incubate the samples at 40 °C for 3 minutes to enhance extraction efficiency.
Homogenize the samples using a TissueLyser at 25 rpm for 60 seconds .
Centrifuge the samples at 14 000 rpm for 5 minutes to pellet debris.
Carefully transfer 500 μL of the supernatant into a new 2.0 mL Eppendorf tube, avoiding any particulate matter.
Evaporate the solvent by drying the samples in a SpeedVac concentrator until completely dry.
Resuspend the dried extract in 2 000 μL of CH 3 CN-H 2 O solution (50:50, v/v)
Sonicate the samples for 15 seconds , followed by vortexing for 30 seconds to ensure complete dissolution.
Centrifuge the samples at 14 000 rpm for 5 minutes to clarify the extract.
Carefully transfer the clarified supernatant into LC-MS vials, avoiding any residual particulates.

Preparation of Sampling Materials and Equipment 50 mL Falcon tubes, a scalpel or knife, scissors, 96 % ethanol for surface sterilization, disposable gloves for handling, a transport box containing dry ice.
Preparation of Materials and Equipment for Extraction EtOH-H 2 O (75:25) CH 3 CN-H 2 O (50:50) 2.0 mL Eppendorf tubes Stainless steel grinding beads Pipettes with tips (100–1000 μL] Labels.
creator: Kateřina Kučerová
